checks.note: CAD worker owns the SolidWorks global lock and passes the lock job id to generation subprocesses.
Batch worker serializes child CAD work under the same SolidWorks global lock contract.
Drawing Review Add-in dimensions are submitted from a QProcess worker after acquiring the lock.
Every Python call that obtains SolidWorks for the Add-in API goes through _get_sw_app lock validation.
Dimension sidecar entrypoint checks the active job lock before invoking C# sidecar or Python COM fallback.
Refdoc relink strategy helpers require the active CAD worker lock before ReplaceViewModel, RunMacro2, sidecar execution, or verification.
v6 generator calls the sidecar from the CAD worker subprocess environment that carries the lock job id.

external_addin_host_lock_contract.interpretation: External Add-in and sidecar source files cannot acquire the Python global lock themselves; their Python host entrypoints must prove QProcess worker routing and current-job lock ownership.

external_addin_needs_host_lock.text: drwModel = swApp.OpenDoc6(drawingPath, 3, 257, "", ref errStatus, ref warnStatus);
partModel = swApp.OpenDoc6(partPath, 1, 257, "", ref errStatus, ref warnStatus);
doc = swApp.OpenDoc6(drawingPath, 3, 257, "", ref err, ref warn);

validation_tool_requires_manual_lock.text: proc = subprocess.run(cmd, stdout=subprocess.PIPE, stderr=subprocess.PIPE, timeout=timeout_s, env=env)
doc = sw.OpenDoc6(str(path), DOC_TYPE_DRAWING, OPEN_SILENT, "", errors, warnings)
